'''若小玉墳群'''（わかこだまこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]にある、古墳群である。 ほとんどの古墳は削平され工業団地になっているが[[八幡山古墳]]と[[地蔵塚古墳]]が現存する。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub|わかこだまこふんくん}}
[[category:古墳|わかこだまこふんくん]]

'''若小玉墳群'''（わかこだまこふんぐん、わくだまこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]にある、古墳群である。
== 概要 == 昭和初期までは愛宕山古墳、荒神山古墳など、3基の[[前方後円墳]]と、8基の[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]が存在。
特徴として、この地域にあった面積の狭い[[ローム (土壌)|ローム]][[台地]]の上に、[[住居]]跡などと共に密集して築かれていることがある。三宝塚古墳は住居跡の上に築造されていた。
現在、ほとんどの古墳は削平され富士見工業団地になっているが[[八幡山古墳 (埼玉県)|八幡山古墳]]と[[地蔵塚古墳 (埼玉県)|地蔵塚古墳]]が残っている。
*[[八幡山古墳 (埼玉県)|八幡山古墳]] - 円墳 *[[地蔵塚古墳 (埼玉県)|地蔵塚古墳]] - 方墳 *愛宕山古墳 - 前方後円墳 *荒神山古墳（こうじんやまこふん） - 前方後円墳 :全長74m。築造年代は6世紀の前半と考えられている。 *笹塚古墳（ささづかこふん） - 円墳 *三宝塚古墳（さんぽうづかこふん） - 前方後円墳 :全長約70m。盾型周溝の外側に造り出しがある<ref>『前方後円墳集成 東北・関東編』616頁 1994年2月</ref>。 *九仙塚古墳（くせんづかこふん） - 円墳 *鎧塚古墳（よろいづかこふん） - 円墳 その他無名の古墳が3基
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉古墳群]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わかこたまこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:行田市の歴史]]

'''若小玉墳群'''（わかこだまこふんぐん、わくだまこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]にある古墳群のひとつ。
== 概要 == [[昭和]]初期までは愛宕山古墳、荒神山古墳など、3基の[[前方後円墳]]と、8基の[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]が存在した。
特徴として、この地域にあった面積の狭い[[ローム (土壌)|ローム]][[台地]]の上に、[[住居]]跡などと共に密集して築かれていることがある。三宝塚古墳は住居跡の上に築造されていた。
現在、ほとんどの古墳は削平され[[富士見工業団地]]になっているが[[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]]と[[地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)|地蔵塚古墳]]が残っている。

'''若小玉墳群'''（わかこだまこふんぐん、わくだまこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]にある古墳群のひとつ。
== 概要 == [[昭和]]初期までは愛宕山古墳、荒神山古墳など、3基の[[前方後円墳]]と、8基の[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]が存在した。現在、ほとんどの古墳は削平され[[富士見工業団地]]になっているが[[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]]と[[地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)|地蔵塚古墳]]が残っている。
特徴として、この地域にあった面積の狭い[[ローム (土壌)|ローム]][[台地]]の上に、[[住居]]跡などと共に密集して築かれていることがある。三宝塚古墳は住居跡の上に築造されていた。
[[埼玉古墳群]]の築造が止んだ後に、新興の豪族が築いたと推定されている<ref>田代脩・塩野博・重田正夫・森田武『埼玉県の歴史』（山川出版社、1994年）、46-47頁。</ref>。
== 古墳の一覧 == * [[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]] - 円墳 * [[地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)|地蔵塚古墳]] - 方墳 * 愛宕山古墳 - 前方後円墳 * 荒神山古墳（こうじんやまこふん） - 前方後円墳 :全長74m。築造年代は[[6世紀]]の前半と考えられている。 * 笹塚古墳（ささづかこふん） - 円墳 * 三宝塚古墳（さんぽうづかこふん） - 前方後円墳 :全長約70m。盾型周溝の外側に[[造り出し]]がある<ref>『前方後円墳集成 東北・関東編』616頁 1994年2月</ref>。 * 九仙塚古墳（くせんづかこふん） - 円墳 * 鎧塚古墳（よろいづかこふん） - 円墳 その他無名の古墳が3基

== 概要 == [[昭和]]初期までは愛宕山古墳、荒神山古墳など、3基の[[前方後円墳]]と、8基の[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]が存在した。現在、ほとんどの古墳は削平され[[富士見工業団地]]になっているが[[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]]と[[地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)|地蔵塚古墳]]が残っている。
特徴として、この地域にあった面積の狭い[[ローム (土壌)|ローム]][[台地]]の上に、[[住居]]跡などと共に密集して築かれていることがある。三宝塚古墳は住居跡の上に築造されていた。
[[埼玉古墳群]]の築造が止んだ後に、新興の豪族が築いたと推定されている<ref>田代脩・塩野博・重田正夫・森田武『埼玉県の歴史』（山川出版社、1994年）、46-47頁。</ref>。

{{日本の古墳 |名称=若小玉古墳群 |画像=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
[[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]]石室開口部 |所在地=[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]藤原町ほか |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=若王子古墳群}} |規模=[[前方後円墳]]3基、[[円墳]]8基・[[方墳]] |築造時期 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]など |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = |史跡 = [[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]]・[[地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)|地蔵塚古墳]]（県指定） |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=若小玉
古墳群|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=埼玉県内の所在地}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord2|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''若小玉墳群'''（わかこだまこふんぐん/わくだまこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]にある[[古墳群]]である。 == 概要 == [[昭和]]初期までは愛宕山古墳、荒神山古墳など、3基の[[前方後円墳]]と、8基の[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]が存在した。現在、ほとんどの古墳は削平され[[富士見工業団地]]になっているが[[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]]と[[地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)|地蔵塚古墳]]が残っている。 特徴として、この地域にあった面積の狭い[[ローム (土壌)|ローム]][[台地]]の上に、[[住居]]跡などと共に密集して築かれていることがある。三宝塚古墳は住居跡の上に築造されていた。 [[埼玉古墳群]]の築造が止んだ後に、新興の豪族が築いたと推定されている{{Sfn|田代|塩野|重田|森田|2010|pp=46-47}}。 == 古墳の一覧 == * [[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]] - 円墳（埼玉県指定[[史跡]]）、「関東の[[石舞台古墳|石舞台]]」とも形容される{{Cite web|author=行田市教育委員会生涯学習部文化財保護課文化財保護担当|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=八幡山古墳石室|work=行田市|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}}。 * [[地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)|地蔵塚古墳]] - 方墳（埼玉県指定史跡） * 愛宕山古墳 - 前方後円墳 * 荒神山古墳（こうじんやまこふん） - 前方後円墳 :全長74メートル。築造年代は[[6世紀]]の前半と考えられている。 * 笹塚古墳（ささづかこふん） - 円墳 * 三宝塚古墳（さんぽうづかこふん） - 前方後円墳 :全長約70メートル。盾型周溝の外側に[[造り出し]]がある{{Sfn|和田晴吾ほか|1994|p=616}}。 * 九仙塚古墳（くせんづかこふん） - 円墳 * 鎧塚古墳（よろいづかこふん） - 円墳 その他無名の古墳が3基 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==参考図書== *{{Cite book|和書|author=[[和田晴吾]]ほか|title=前方後円墳集成 東北・関東編|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|year=1994|date=1994-02-01|page=616|isbn=4634500108|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|和書|last=田代|first=脩|last2=塩野|first2=博|last3=重田|first3=正夫|last4=森田|first4=武|title=埼玉県の歴史|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|series=県史11|year=2010|date=2010-11-01|page=46|isbn=4634321114|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉古墳群]] == 外部リンク == *{{Cite web|author=[[埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館]]|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=周辺の古墳案内|work=埼玉県|accessdate=2020-07-18}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わかこたまこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:行田市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

revision.comment: Botによる: [[Special:Permalink/83473788#Maplink2とOSM Location mapテンプレートに渡す引数におけるCoord2テンプレートをCoordテンプレートに置換する|Bot作業依頼]] {{[[Template:Maplink2|Maplink2]]}}と{{[[Template:OSM Location map|OSM Location map]]}}に渡す座標を{{[[Template:Coord2|Coord2]]}}から{{[[Template:Coord|Coord]]}}に変更
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=若小玉古墳群 |画像=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
[[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]]石室開口部 |所在地=[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]藤原町ほか |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=若王子古墳群}} |規模=[[前方後円墳]]3基、[[円墳]]8基・[[方墳]] |築造時期 = |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]など |被葬者 = 不明 |出土品 = |史跡 = [[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]]・[[地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)|地蔵塚古墳]]（県指定） |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=若小玉
古墳群|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=埼玉県内の所在地}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''若小玉墳群'''（わかこだまこふんぐん/わくだまこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]にある[[古墳群]]である。 == 概要 == [[昭和]]初期までは愛宕山古墳、荒神山古墳など、3基の[[前方後円墳]]と、8基の[[円墳]]・[[方墳]]が存在した。現在、ほとんどの古墳は削平され[[富士見工業団地]]になっているが[[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]]と[[地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)|地蔵塚古墳]]が残っている。 特徴として、この地域にあった面積の狭い[[ローム (土壌)|ローム]][[台地]]の上に、[[住居]]跡などと共に密集して築かれていることがある。三宝塚古墳は住居跡の上に築造されていた。 [[埼玉古墳群]]の築造が止んだ後に、新興の豪族が築いたと推定されている{{Sfn|田代|塩野|重田|森田|2010|pp=46-47}}。 == 古墳の一覧 == * [[八幡山古墳 (行田市)|八幡山古墳]] - 円墳（埼玉県指定[[史跡]]）、「関東の[[石舞台古墳|石舞台]]」とも形容される{{Cite web|author=行田市教育委員会生涯学習部文化財保護課文化財保護担当|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=八幡山古墳石室|work=行田市|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}}。 * [[地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)|地蔵塚古墳]] - 方墳（埼玉県指定史跡） * 愛宕山古墳 - 前方後円墳 * 荒神山古墳（こうじんやまこふん） - 前方後円墳 :全長74メートル。築造年代は[[6世紀]]の前半と考えられている。 * 笹塚古墳（ささづかこふん） - 円墳 * 三宝塚古墳（さんぽうづかこふん） - 前方後円墳 :全長約70メートル。盾型周溝の外側に[[造り出し]]がある{{Sfn|和田晴吾ほか|1994|p=616}}。 * 九仙塚古墳（くせんづかこふん） - 円墳 * 鎧塚古墳（よろいづかこふん） - 円墳 その他無名の古墳が3基 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==参考図書== *{{Cite book|和書|author=[[和田晴吾]]ほか|title=前方後円墳集成 東北・関東編|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|year=1994|date=1994-02-01|page=616|isbn=4634500108|ref=harv}} *{{Cite book|和書|last=田代|first=脩|last2=塩野|first2=博|last3=重田|first3=正夫|last4=森田|first4=武|title=埼玉県の歴史|publisher=[[山川出版社]]|series=県史11|year=2010|date=2010-11-01|page=46|isbn=4634321114|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉古墳群]] == 外部リンク == *{{Cite web|author=[[埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館]]|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=周辺の古墳案内|work=埼玉県|accessdate=2020-07-18}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わかこたまこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:行田市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |Image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of [[Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)]] |Location=[[Gyoda]], [[Saitama Prefecture]] |Coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 [[zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 [[empun]] (round kofun), and [[hōfun]] (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities=[[Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage=[[Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)]] and [[Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)]] (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyoda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs (前方後円墳), eight round tombs (円墳), and several square tombs (方墳), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, [[Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)]] and [[Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)]] remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * [[Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)]] - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * [[Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)]] - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * [[List of kofun in Japan]] * [[List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] * [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]

The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:K]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]若小玉古墳群]]

The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyoda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:K]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] 若小玉古墳群]]

Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyoda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama {{Infobox ancient site |Name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:new pages]][[ja: [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]] [[ja: [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:new pages]] [[ja: [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:new pages]] [[ja: [[Category:K]][[Category:new pages]] [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[Category:new pages]] [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[Category:new pages]] [[ja: [[Category:new pages]] [[ja: [[Category:new pages]] [[ja: [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:若小玉古墳群]]

{{Infobox ancient site |Name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[ja: [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[ja: [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[ja: [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[ja: [[Category:K]] [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja: [[ja: [[ja: [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:若小玉古墳群]]

While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[ja: [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[ja: [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[ja: [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[ja: [[Category:K]] [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja: [[ja: [[ja: [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:若小玉古墳群]]

Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[ja: [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[ja: [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[ja: [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[ja: [[Category:K]] [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja: [[ja: [[ja: [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:若小玉古墳群]]

{{Infobox ancient site |Name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[ja: [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[ja: [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[ja: [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[ja: [[Category:K]] [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja: [[ja: [[ja: [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[ja:若小玉古墳群]]

Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[ja: [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[ja: [[Category:History of Gyoda]] [[ja: [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[ja: [[Category:K]] [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja: [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja: [[ja: [[ja: [[ja: [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Wakakodama Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:若小玉古墳群]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama
