revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{citation needed|Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{citation needed|Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{citation needed|Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: aqqroximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: aqqroximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: aqqroximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: aqqroximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: aqqroximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mounds) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: aqqroximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: aqqroximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: aqqroximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama
