{{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: aqqroximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shuqqansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: a11roximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: a11roximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Kofun|work=Saitama Prefecture}} {{citation needed|Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |Scale=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |Map2={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |map_size=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |Findings= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |map={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |map_size=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |Construction Era= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Buried Persons=Unknown |burial goods= |Cultural Heritage={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |map={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |map_size=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |built= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Interred_individual=Unknown |burial goods= |other_designation={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |map={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |map_size=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |built= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Interred_individual=Unknown |burial goods= |other_designation={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |map={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |map_size=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |built= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Interred_individual=Unknown |burial goods= |other_designation={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |map={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |map_size=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |built= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Interred_individual=Unknown |burial goods= |other_designation={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |map={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |map_size=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |built= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Interred_individual=Unknown |burial goods= |other_designation={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |map={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural Historic Site); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural Historic Site) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster |image=[[File:Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda), entrance.JPG|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber of {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} |location={{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}} |coordinates={{Coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Wakakodama Kofun Cluster}} |map_size=3 {{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped kofun), 8 {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (round kofun), and {{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (square kofun) |built= |Burial Facilities={{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} and others |Interred_individual=Unknown |burial goods= |other_designation={{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} (Prefectural Designation) |map={{location map|Japan Saitama|label=Wakakodama
Kofun Cluster|lat_deg=36|lat_min=08|lat_sec=34.3|lon_deg=139|lon_min=29|lon_sec=12.6|mark=|marksize=8|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Saitama Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|08|34.3|N|139|29|12.6|E}}}} }} '''Wakakodama Kofun Cluster''' (若小玉古墳群, Wakakodama Kofun Cluster) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Gyōda|ja|行田市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan. == Overview == As of the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster consisted of three keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), eight round tombs ({{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), and several square tombs ({{ill|hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), including prominent examples such as Atagoyama Kofun and Kojinyama Kofun. While most of the kofun have been leveled to make way for the {{ill|Fujimi Industrial Complex|ja|富士見工業団地}}, {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} and {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} remain intact. The kofun in this cluster are characterized by their dense distribution atop the region's narrow loam plateau, where they coexisted with evidence of residential sites. Notably, the Sanpōzuka Kofun was built directly over a residential site. The construction of the Wakakodama Kofun Cluster is believed to have taken place after the cessation of tomb construction in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}, likely reflecting the rise of a new local ruling family.{{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == List of Kofun == * {{ill|Hachiman-yama Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|八幡山古墳 (行田市)}} - Round tomb (Prefectural other_designation); also referred to as "The Stone Stage of the Kanto."{{Cite web|author=Gyoda City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division|url=https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/41/03/10/bunkazai_itiran/hatimanyamakofunsekisitu.html|title=Hachiman-yama Kofun Stone Chamber|work=Gyoda City|date=2015-11-02|accessdate=2020-07-16}} * {{ill|Jizōzuka Kofun (Gyoda)|ja|地蔵塚古墳 (行田市)}} - Square tomb (Prefectural other_designation) * Atagoyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb * Kojinyama Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: 74 meters; construction era: early 6th century * Sasazuka Kofun - Round tomb * Sanpōzuka Kofun - Keyhole-shaped tomb : Total length: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 70 meters; features a tategata moats and external protrusions.{{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * Kusen-zuka Kofun - Round tomb * Yoroizuka Kofun - Round tomb Three additional unnamed tombs are present in the area. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=Wada Harugo|title=Zenpokoenfun Shūsei: Tohoku and Kanto Vol.|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=1994|page=616}} * {{Cite book|last=Tashiro|title=Saitama no Rekishi|publisher=Yamakawa Shu11ansha|year=2010|pages=46–47}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|author=Saitama Prefectural Museum of Sakitama|url=https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/%E5%9F%BC%E7%8E%89%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E7%BE%A4%E7%B4%B9%E4%BB%8B/%E5%91%A8%E8%BE%BA%E3%81%AE%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3%E6%A1%88%E5%86%85|title=Guide to Nearby
